volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Oedipus at Colonus (Section 2.1096 - Section 1.1230)

sections.label: Section 2.1096
sections.original: ὡς ψευδόμαντις · τὰς κόρας γὰρ εἰσορῶ
sections.translation: Wandering stranger , you will not say your watcher was a false prophet , for I see your daughters once again drawing near .

sections.label: Section 1099
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ πάτερ ,
sections.translation: Where ? Where ? What is that ? What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 1100
sections.original: τίς ἂν θεῶν σοι τόνδʼ ἄριστον ἄνδρʼ ἰδεῖν
sections.translation: I wish some god would grant that your eyes might see this excellent man , who has brought us here to you !

sections.label: Section 1102
sections.original: ὦ τέκνον , ἦ πάρεστον ;
sections.translation: My child , are you really here ?

sections.label: Section 1102a
sections.original: αἵδε γὰρ χέρες
sections.translation: Yes , for these strong arms have saved us — Theseus and his dearest followers .

sections.label: Section 1104
sections.original: προσέλθετʼ ὦ παῖ , πατρὶ καὶ τὸ μηδαμὰ
sections.translation: Come here , my children , to your father !

sections.label: Section 1105
sections.original: ἐλπισθὲν ἥξειν σῶμα βαστάσαι δότε .
sections.translation: Grant me your embrace — restored beyond all hope !

sections.label: Section 1106
sections.original: αἰτεῖς ἃ τεύξει · σὺν πόθῳ γὰρ ἡ χάρις .
sections.translation: We shall grant your wish , for we crave the favor we bestow .

sections.label: Section 1107
sections.original: ποῦ δῆτα , ποῦ ʼστόν ;
sections.translation: Where , then , where are you ?

sections.label: Section 1107a
sections.original: αἵδʼ ὁμοῦ πελάζομεν .
sections.translation: Here we are , approaching you together .

sections.label: Section 1108
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατʼ ἔρνη .
sections.translation: Dearest offspring !

sections.label: Section 1108a
sections.original: τῷ τεκόντι πᾶν φίλον .
sections.translation: Everything is dear to its parent .

sections.label: Section 1109
sections.original: ὦ σκῆπτρα φωτός .
sections.translation: Supports of a man —

sections.label: Section 1109a
sections.original: δυσμόρου γε δύσμορα .
sections.translation: Ill-fated as he is ill-fated .

sections.label: Section 1110
sections.original: ἔχω τὰ φίλτατʼ , οὐδʼ ἔτʼ ἂν πανάθλιος
sections.translation: I hold my dearest . Now , if I should die , I would not be wholly wretched , since you have come to me . Press close to me on either side , children , cling to your father , and rest from your wandering , so desolate , so grievous !

sections.label: Section 1115
sections.original: καί μοι τὰ πραχθέντʼ εἴπαθʼ ὡς βράχιστʼ , ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: And tell me what has happened as briefly as you can , since brief speech suffices for young maidens .

sections.label: Section 1117
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἔσθʼ ὁ σώσας · τοῦδε χρὴ κλύειν , πάτερ ,
sections.translation: Here is our savior : you should hear the story from him , father , since the deed was his . So short will by part be .

sections.label: Section 1119
sections.original: ὦ ξεῖνε , μὴ θαύμαζε , πρὸς τὸ λιπαρὲς
sections.translation: Stranger , do not be amazed at my persistence , if I prolong my words to my children ,

sections.label: Section 1120
sections.original: τέκνʼ εἰ φανέντʼ ἄελπτα μηκύνω λόγον .
sections.translation: found again beyond my hope . I well know that my present joy in them has come to me from you , and you alone , for you — and not any other mortal — have rescued them . May the gods grant to you my wish ,

sections.label: Section 1125
sections.original: αὐτῷ τε καὶ γῇ τῇδʼ , ἐπεὶ τό γʼ εὐσεβὲς
sections.translation: both to you yourself and to this land ; for among you , above all mankind , I have found piety , the spirit of decency , and lips that tell no lie . I know these things , and I repay them with these words ; for what I have , I have through you , and no one else .

sections.label: Section 1130
sections.original: καί μοι χέρʼ , ὦναξ , δεξιὰν ὄρεξον , ὡς
sections.translation: Stretch out to me your right hand , lord , that I may touch it ; and if it is right , let me kiss your cheek . But what am I saying ? Wretched as I have become , how could I wish you to touch a man in whom every stain of evils has made its dwelling ?

sections.label: Section 1135
sections.original: οὐδʼ οὖν ἐάσω · τοῖς γὰρ ἐμπείροις βροτῶν
sections.translation: I will not touch you — nor will I allow it , if you do consent . They alone , who know them , can share these burdens . Receive my greeting where you stand , and in the future too give me your righteous care , as you have given it up to this hour .

sections.label: Section 1139
sections.original: οὔτʼ εἴ τι μῆκος τῶν λόγων ἔθου πλέον ,
sections.translation: I feel no amazement , if you have had a lengthy conversation

sections.label: Section 1140
sections.original: τέκνοισι τερφθεὶς τοῖσδε , θαυμάσας ἔχω ,
sections.translation: from joy in these children , or if your first concern has been for their words rather than for me . Indeed , there is nothing to vex me in that . Not with words so much as with deeds would I add luster to my life . You have this proof :

sections.label: Section 1145
sections.original: δείκνυμι δʼ · ὧν γὰρ ὤμοσʼ οὐκ ἐψευσάμην
sections.translation: I have cheated you in none of my sworn promises , old man . Here am I , with the maidens living , uninjured by those threats . As to how the struggle was won , what need have I vainly to boast of what you will learn from these two when you are together ?

sections.label: Section 1150
sections.original: λόγος δʼ ὃς ἐμπέπτωκεν ἀρτίως ἐμοὶ
sections.translation: But there is a matter that has just presented itself to me , as I came here . Give me your counsel regarding it ; for , though it is small , it is food for wonder . And mortal man must consider nothing beneath his concern .

sections.label: Section 1155
sections.original: ὡς μὴ εἰδότʼ αὐτὸν μηδὲν ὧν σὺ πυνθάνει .
sections.translation: What is it , son of Aegeus ? Tell me ; I myself know nothing of what you inquire .

sections.label: Section 1156
sections.original: φασίν τινʼ ἡμῖν ἄνδρα , σοὶ μὲν ἔμπολιν
sections.translation: They say a man — not from your city , yet of your race — has somehow thrown himself down , as a suppliant , at our altar of Poseidon , where I was sacrificing when I first set out here .

sections.label: Section 1160
sections.original: ποδαπόν ; τί προσχρῄζοντα τῷ θακήματι ·
sections.translation: What land does he come form ? What does he desire by his supplication ?

sections.label: Section 1161
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδα πλὴν ἕν · σοῦ γάρ , ὡς λέγουσί μοι ,
sections.translation: I know one thing only : they tell me he asks to speak briefly with you , a thing of no great burden .

sections.label: Section 1163
sections.original: ποῖόν τινʼ ; οὐ γὰρ ἥδʼ ἕδρα σμικροῦ λόγου .
sections.translation: On what topic ? That suppliant state is of no small account .

sections.label: Section 1164
sections.original: σοὶ φασὶν αὐτὸν ἐς λόγους ἐλθεῖν μόνον
sections.translation: He asks , they say , no more than that he may confer with you ,

sections.label: Section 1165
sections.original: αἰτεῖν ἀπελθεῖν τʼ ἀσφαλῶς τῆς δεῦρʼ ὁδοῦ .
sections.translation: and return unharmed from his journey here .

sections.label: Section 1166
sections.original: τίς δῆτʼ ἂν εἴη τήνδʼ ὁ προσθακῶν ἕδραν ;
sections.translation: Who can he be that implores the god in this way ?

sections.label: Section 1167
sections.original: ὅρα κατʼ Ἄργος εἴ τις ὑμὶν ἐγγενὴς
sections.translation: Consider whether there is anyone in your race at Argos , who might desire this favor from you .

sections.label: Section 1169
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατε , σχὲς οὗπερ εἶ .
sections.translation: Dearest friend , say no more !

sections.label: Section 1169a
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι σοι ;
sections.translation: What is wrong ?

sections.label: Section 1170
sections.original: μή μου δεηθῇς .
sections.translation: Do not ask me for —

sections.label: Section 1170a
sections.original: πράγματος ποίου ; λέγε .
sections.translation: For what ? Speak !

sections.label: Section 1171
sections.original: ἔξοιδʼ ἀκούων τῶνδʼ ὅς ἐσθʼ ὁ προστάτης .
sections.translation: From hearing these things I know who the suppliant is .

sections.label: Section 1172
sections.original: καὶ τίς ποτʼ ἐστὶν ὅν γʼ ἐγὼ ψέξαιμί τι ;
sections.translation: And who can he be , that I should have an objection to him ?

sections.label: Section 1173
sections.original: παῖς οὑμός , ὦναξ , στυγνός , οὗ λόγων ἐγὼ
sections.translation: My son , lord , a hated son whose words would vex my ear like the words of no man besides .

sections.label: Section 1175
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐκ ἀκούειν ἔστι καὶ μὴ δρᾶν ἃ μὴ
sections.translation: What ? Can you not listen , without doing what you do not wish to do ? Why does it pain you to hear him ?

sections.label: Section 1177
sections.original: ἔχθιστον , ὦναξ , φθέγμα τοῦθʼ ἥκει πατρί ·
sections.translation: Lord , that voice has become most hateful to his father . Do not constrain me to yield in this .

sections.label: Section 1179
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ τὸ θάκημʼ ἐξαναγκάζει , σκόπει
sections.translation: But consider whether his suppliant state constrains you ;

sections.label: Section 1180
sections.original: μή σοι πρόνοιʼ ᾖ τοῦ θεοῦ φυλακτέα .
sections.translation: what if you have a duty of respect for the god ?

sections.label: Section 1181
sections.original: πάτερ , πιθοῦ μοι , κεἰ νέα παραινέσω .
sections.translation: Father , listen to me ; I will offer counsel though I am young . Allow this man here to gratify his own feelings and the god as he wishes , and for your daughters ’ sake allow our brother to come .

sections.label: Section 1185
sections.original: καὶ νῷν ὕπεικε τὸν κασίγνητον μολεῖν .
sections.translation: He will not tear you by force from your resolve — never fear — with such words as will not be for your good . What harm can there be in listening to words ? Deeds wickedly devised , as you know , are betrayed by speech . You sired him ,

sections.label: Section 1190
sections.original: ἔφυσας αὐτόν · ὥστε μηδὲ δρῶντά σε
sections.translation:
so , even if he wrongs you with the most impious of wrongs , father , it is not right for you to wrong him in return .
Let him come ! Other men too have evil offspring and a sharp anger , but they hear advice and are charmed from their mood by the gentle spells of friends .

sections.label: Section 1195
sections.original: φίλων ἐπῳδαῖς ἐξεπᾴδονται φύσιν .
sections.translation: Look to the past , away from the present ; consider all the pains that you have suffered through your father and mother . If you consider those things , I know well that you will perceive that what results from an evil anger is evil . Your reasons to reflect on this are not trivial ,

sections.label: Section 1200
sections.original: ἔχεις γὰρ οὐχὶ βαιὰ τἀνθυμήματα ,
sections.translation:
bereft of your unseeing eyes .
Yield to us ! It is not a fine thing for those seeking justice to keep asking ; nor is it good that a man should be treated well , and thereafter not know how to requite it .

sections.label: Section 1204
sections.original: πάσχειν , παθόντα δʼ οὐκ ἐπίστασθαι τίνειν .
sections.translation: My child , by your pleading you overcome me ; but your pleasure here is my grief .

sections.label: Section 1205
sections.original: τέκνον , βαρεῖαν ἡδονὴν νικᾶτέ με
sections.translation: Still , let it be as is dear to you . Only , if that man is to come here , stranger , let no one ever become master over my soul .

sections.label: Section 1208
sections.original: μηδεὶς κρατείτω τῆς ἐμῆς ψυχῆς ποτε .
sections.translation: Once only do I need hear such words , and no more , old man . I do not want to boast ,

sections.label: Section 1210
sections.original: σῶς ἴσθʼ , ἐάν περ κἀμέ τις σῴζῃ θεῶν .
sections.translation: but you may feel sure that your life is safe , while any of the gods preserves mine . Theseus exits .

sections.label: Section 1.1211
sections.original: ὅστις τοῦ πλέονος μέρους χρῄζει τοῦ μετρίου παρεὶς
sections.translation: Whoever craves the longer length of life , not content to desire a moderate span , him I will judge with no uncertainty : he clings to folly .

sections.label: Section 1.1215
sections.original: ἐπεὶ πολλὰ μὲν αἱ μακραὶ ἁμέραι κατέθεντο δὴ
sections.translation: For the long years lay in deposit many things nearer to pain than joy ; but as for your delights , you will find them nowhere , when someone ’ s life has fallen beyond the fitting period .

sections.label: Section 1.1220
sections.original: τοῦ δέοντος · ὁ δʼ ἐπίκουρος ἰσοτέλεστος ,
sections.translation: The Helper comes at last to all alike , when the fate of Hades is suddenly revealed , without marriage-song , or lyre , or dance : Death at the end .

sections.label: Section 1.1225
sections.original: μὴ φῦναι τὸν ἅπαντα νικᾷ λόγον · τὸ δʼ , ἐπεὶ φανῇ ,
sections.translation:
Not to be born is , beyond all estimation , best ; but when a man has seen the light of day , this is next best by far , that with utmost speed he should go back from where he came .
For when he has seen youth go by , with its easy merry-making ,

sections.label: Section 1.1230
sections.original: ὡς εὖτʼ ἂν τὸ νέον παρῇ κούφας ἀφροσύνας φέρον ,
sections.translation: what hard affliction is foreign to him , what suffering does he not know ? Envy , factions , strife , battles ,
